old_claim.note: Loom provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: The scoped warp-weighted loom is identified from its grouped tension weights, making those weights constitutive components of this reconstruction.

source_shape.source_locator: Ulucak discussion reports eleven loom weights together in Building 12 and treats that cluster as the clearest Neolithic loom evidence in Anatolia.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The inferred warp-weighted loom is archaeologically reconstructed from a spatially associated set of eleven surviving tension weights.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The weights are physical components used to infer the loom, so component-to-system direction is supported while system-to-component direction is circular.

ontology_before: A loom weight depended on an already completed loom, creating a circular component relationship.
ontology_after: The reconstructed warp-weighted loom requires the surviving tension-weight components that identify it.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: loom now requires loom_weights, and loom_weights no longer depends on loom.
would_reject_if: The Building 12 objects were reidentified as unrelated weights with no role in the loom reconstruction.
short_reason: The surviving weights are components and evidence of the loom, not its downstream product.
